key_insight: A non-overlapping domain decomposition method turns the coupled Stokes-Biot fluid–poroelastic system into a parallelizable interface problem solved efficiently with GMRES and Lagrange multipliers.
engineering_relevance: This enables scalable simulation of fluid-structure interactions in porous media, relevant to biomechanics, geomechanics, and other multi-domain engineering systems.
method: Split the global Stokes-Biot model into Stokes and Biot subdomains with Stokes-Stokes, Biot-Biot, and Stokes-Biot interfaces.
Enforce interface physics weakly using Lagrange multipliers for velocity, displacement, pressure, and normal stress continuity.
Reduce the coupled PDE system via Schur complement to an interface operator and solve it with GMRES in parallel local subproblems.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments and a fields-of-value analysis showing the interface operator is positive definite and GMRES convergence can be characterized.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of the mixed finite element discretization and the cost of repeated local Stokes/Biot solves at each GMRES iteration.
paper_title: Domain decomposition methods for the Stokes-Biot model of fluid-poroelastic structure interaction
